Alabama.severity_quote: Multiple leaves show extensive yellowing, large brown necrotic patches at the leaf margins, and shot-hole/perforations where necrotic tissue has dropped out.
Alabama.lesion_morphology: Irregular brown-to-tan necrotic blotches concentrated along leaf margins and tips, surrounded by broad chlorotic yellow halos, with small dark flecks scattered across the laminae and several coalesced areas that have torn out into ragged holes.
Alabama.lesion_morphology_quote: Necrotic patches are dry, tan-brown with diffuse yellow borders, and the centers have fallen out leaving irregular holes in the leaflets.
Alabama.affected_organs_quote: Only soybean leaflets are visible, showing widespread chlorosis, marginal necrosis, and shot-hole damage; petioles, stems, and pods are not clearly shown.
Alabama.spread_pattern_quote: Nearly every leaflet in the frame shows symptoms, with damage distributed across the entire visible canopy rather than localized to a single region.
variations_from_canonical: The dominant discoloration here is yellow chlorosis transitioning to tan/brown necrosis, not the reddish-purple or bronze 'sunburned' tone the canonical description emphasizes.
Lesions have coalesced and dropped out into shot-holes/perforations, a level of tissue loss not described in the canonical reference.
Damage is concentrated at leaf margins and tips rather than as discrete purple spots on the upper leaf surface.
No reddish-purple lesions on petioles, stems, or pods are visible in this image, though the canonical text lists those as diagnostic.

Illinois.severity_quote: A large soybean leaflet shows widespread chlorosis with numerous coalescing brown necrotic flecks across most of the upper surface, indicating advanced late-season infection.
Illinois.lesion_morphology: Small, irregular, dark reddish-brown to chocolate-brown pinpoint flecks and angular spots, mostly 1-3 mm, scattered densely across a yellow-green chlorotic background, with some lesions coalescing into larger necrotic patches near the leaf margin and tip.
Illinois.lesion_morphology_quote: The leaflet is peppered with small dark brown specks against a mottled yellow-green background, with browning concentrating along the upper margin and tip.
Illinois.affected_organs_quote: Only a soybean trifoliate leaflet is in focus, showing dense brown stippling and chlorotic discoloration on the upper surface; petioles, stems, and pods are not clearly visible.
Illinois.spread_pattern_quote: The brown flecks are distributed fairly evenly across the entire upper leaf surface, with heavier concentration toward the leaf tip and margin.
variations_from_canonical: The discoloration in this image is yellow-chlorotic and brown rather than the canonical 'light purple' / 'reddish-purple' / 'bronze' upper-surface color.
Symptoms appear as discrete small brown flecks and pinpoint spots rather than a diffuse leathery 'sunburned' purple sheen described as canonical.
No reddish-purple lesions are visible on petioles, stems, or pods — only the leaflet is shown, so the canonical multi-organ involvement is not demonstrated here.
Lesion color trends toward chocolate-brown necrotic specks rather than the purple/bronze cercosporin-driven discoloration emphasized in the canonical description.

Alabama.severity_quote: Multiple trifoliate leaves across the canopy show distinct light-green/yellow mottling patches, but leaves remain largely intact and a flower is present, indicating an active but not yet advanced infection.
Alabama.lesion_morphology: Diffuse, irregular pale-green to light-yellow mottled blotches scattered across the upper leaf surfaces, without sharply defined margins, accompanied by some small dark feeding holes in several leaflets.
Alabama.lesion_morphology_quote: Pale chlorotic patches are interspersed with darker green tissue producing a blotchy mosaic, with no necrotic lesion borders visible.
Alabama.affected_organs_quote: Mottling is visible across numerous trifoliate leaflets in the upper canopy; pods and seeds are not visible in the frame.
Alabama.spread_pattern_quote: Mottling appears on most leaves throughout the visible upper canopy rather than being confined to a single area or row edge.
variations_from_canonical: No leaf puckering, rugosity, or pronounced distortion is visible — leaflets appear relatively flat and normally shaped despite the chlorotic mottling described in canonical literature.
No pods, seeds, or stems are clearly shown, so the canonical pod/seed mottling and 'green stems after pod maturity' diagnostic features cannot be evaluated from this image.
A purple flower is visible, suggesting the plant is at flowering rather than late pod-fill — earlier in the canonical disease timeline than the seed/pod symptoms described.
Several small holes consistent with insect feeding are visible on multiple leaves, which is not part of the canonical viral symptom description.

Iowa.severity_quote: A trifoliate leaf shows pronounced green-to-yellow mottling and surface puckering across most of the lamina, with healthy darker leaves visible elsewhere in the canopy.
Iowa.lesion_morphology: Diffuse blotchy chlorotic patches with irregular margins interspersed with darker green islands, accompanied by a rugose (puckered/wrinkled) leaf surface; no discrete spots or necrotic lesions are present.
Iowa.lesion_morphology_quote: Yellow-green and pale-green mottled zones blend gradually into darker green tissue, and the leaf surface appears blistered/rugose between veins.
Iowa.affected_organs_quote: Mottling, chlorosis, and rugosity are visible on the trifoliate leaflets; no stems, pods, or seeds are shown in the frame.
Iowa.spread_pattern_quote: Symptoms are concentrated on the foreground upper-canopy trifoliate while surrounding background foliage looks comparatively dark green and asymptomatic.
variations_from_canonical: Image shows only foliar symptoms; pods, stems, and seeds described in canonical text are not visible.
A few small holes/notches in the leaf are present (likely insect feeding) — not part of the canonical BPMV description.
No plant stunting can be assessed from this close-up frame.

Alabama.severity_quote: A single soybean leaf held in a hand is densely peppered with dozens of coalescing spots covering most of the lamina, and surrounding leaves in the canopy show similar heavy speckling.
Alabama.lesion_morphology: Numerous small round-to-irregular spots (~1-3 mm) with pale tan-to-gray centers and dark reddish-brown margins; many lesions have dropped out leaving small shot-holes, and several have coalesced into larger angular brown necrotic patches.
Alabama.lesion_morphology_quote: Small circular spots with light gray-tan centers ringed by reddish-brown borders are scattered across the leaf, with shot-hole perforations and a few merged necrotic blotches near the leaf margin.
Alabama.affected_organs_quote: Only foliage is shown — a held trifoliate leaflet and adjacent canopy leaves all bear the spotting; no stems, pods, or seeds are visible.
Alabama.spread_pattern: uniform across leaf, widespread in canopy
Alabama.spread_pattern_quote: The lesions are distributed evenly from midrib to margin on the held leaf and similar speckling is visible on neighboring leaves in the background.
variations_from_canonical: Lesion density is far heavier than the typical 'small dark spots' description — dozens of coalescing spots per leaflet rather than discrete scattered ones.
Many lesions have progressed to shot-hole perforations (centers dropped out), which is not mentioned in the canonical description.
Centers appear pale tan to grayish rather than the canonical 'light brown or gray', and the dark surrounding ring reads as reddish-brown but not distinctly purple.
No black pepper-like fungal fruiting bodies are clearly resolvable in the centers at this image scale.
Image only documents foliar symptoms; no stem, pod, or seed lesions are visible despite canonical mention of those organs.

Illinois.severity_quote: The leaf has extensive yellowing/chlorosis with dense brown speckling and multiple coalescing lesions on a senescing leaf, indicating advanced late-season infection.
Illinois.lesion_morphology: Small to medium round spots with gray-tan centers and dark reddish-brown to purple rings, scattered among numerous smaller dark brown peppery flecks across a yellowing leaf surface.
Illinois.lesion_morphology_quote: Multiple circular lesions show pale gray centers ringed by dark purple-brown borders, surrounded by abundant fine dark stippling on chlorotic leaf tissue.
Illinois.affected_organs_quote: A single trifoliate soybean leaf is shown bearing both ringed eyespot lesions and dense brown speckling; no stems, pods, or seeds are visible in detail.
Illinois.spread_pattern_quote: Discrete ringed lesions are scattered across the lamina while finer brown specks are diffusely spread over most of the leaf surface.
variations_from_canonical: Leaf is heavily chlorotic/senescing yellow rather than the green leaf background implied by the canonical description.
In addition to the canonical ringed eyespots, the image shows abundant fine peppery brown stippling across the leaf, more diffuse than the discrete 1-5 mm spots described.
Lesion centers appear gray-tan and somewhat sunken/aged rather than the light brown described for younger lesions.
Only foliar symptoms are visible; no stem, pod, or seed lesions are shown in this single image.

Alabama.severity_quote: The plant has been uprooted with desiccated roots and a bleached, girdled lower stem, indicating the plant is dead or near-dead from infection.
Alabama.lesion_morphology: A pale tan to bleached, dry-looking lesion encircles the lower stem near the soil line, with a few small dark brown specks scattered on the bleached bark and no clearly visible white mycelial mat.
Alabama.lesion_morphology_quote: Lower stem shows a bleached tan band with scattered dark pinhead-sized flecks rather than a continuous fungal mat.
Alabama.affected_organs_quote: The lower stem is bleached and girdled and the root mass above it is dried and clogged with soil; the entire plant has been pulled and is dead.
Alabama.spread_pattern_quote: The frame shows one excavated soybean plant held up against a green field backdrop, so canopy-level spread cannot be assessed.
variations_from_canonical: The conspicuous white, fan-like mycelial mat described in the canonical reference is not visible on the stem, leaf residue, or soil in this image.
Sclerotia are sparse and inconspicuous — only a few small dark flecks are visible on the stem, not the abundant mustard-seed-like clusters the canonical description emphasizes.
Lesion appears as a dry, bleached/tan band rather than the 'dark brown girdling lesion' described at maturity in the canonical text.
No attached brown leaves are visible on the displayed stem, unlike the canonical description of leaves turning brown and remaining attached.

Arkansas.severity_quote: Multiple soybean stems at the soil line are bleached white and girdled, with extensive white mycelial growth and the lower canopy already collapsing into brown, withered foliage.
Arkansas.lesion_morphology: Bleached, light tan to white girdling lesions wrapping the lower stems just above the soil line, with fluffy white fan-like mycelial mats spreading onto adjacent soil and plant debris, and a few small tan-brown sclerotia visible on the colonized tissue.
Arkansas.lesion_morphology_quote: Stems near the soil are uniformly whitened and dried, coated in cottony white fungal growth, with small rounded tan sclerotia adhering to the stem surface.
Arkansas.affected_organs_quote: Lower stems show bleaching and white mycelial mats, leaves above are wilted and brown, and white mycelium has spread out across the soil and crop residue.
Arkansas.spread_pattern: lower canopy / row-base, multiple adjacent plants
Arkansas.spread_pattern_quote: Several neighboring stems within the same row segment are simultaneously colonized at the base, with mycelium bridging across the soil between them.
variations_from_canonical: Stems in this image are bleached white rather than the canonical 'light brown' progressing to 'dark brown girdling' — the lesions look more dried/whitened than darkened.
Sclerotia are sparse and pale tan in this view rather than the canonical 'numerous' reddish-brown to dark-brown mature sclerotia.
Damage spans multiple adjacent plants rather than appearing as scattered seedling damping-off, suggesting later-season row-level collapse.

Alabama.severity_quote: Two detached leaflets held in a hand show heavy, dense lesion coverage with extensive chlorosis and tan-brown coalescing necrosis, particularly on the left leaflet which is largely discolored.
Alabama.lesion_morphology: Numerous small tan to reddish-brown angular spots densely peppered across the leaf surface, with broad yellowing zones and patches where lesions have coalesced into larger dusty brown necrotic areas; a few small perforations are also present.
Alabama.lesion_morphology_quote: The right leaflet displays a fine stippling of tan/rust-brown angular flecks against a yellow-green background, while the left leaflet shows the same lesions merged into a uniform tan-brown discoloration.
Alabama.affected_organs_quote: Only foliage (two soybean leaflets) is visible in the photograph; no stems or pods are shown.
Alabama.spread_pattern_quote: Lesions are distributed densely and fairly evenly across the entire lamina of both leaflets rather than clustered along veins or margins.
variations_from_canonical: Image shows an advanced/late-stage presentation with widespread chlorosis and coalesced necrosis, beyond the canonical 'small 2-5 mm angular lesions' early description.
Volcano-shaped uredinia and salmon/tan urediniospore masses are not resolvable at this image scale — only surface stippling is visible.
Canopy position cannot be confirmed because the leaflets have been detached and held up for the photograph rather than shown in situ in the lower canopy.
Left leaflet's damage pattern looks more like diffuse bronzing/coalesced necrosis than the discrete angular 2-5 mm lesions described canonically.

Florida.severity_quote: The underside of the leaf is densely peppered with tan-orange pustules across nearly the entire blade, indicating heavy sporulation.
Florida.lesion_morphology: Numerous fine, pinpoint tan to rust-brown pustules distributed densely across the leaf underside, with several small necrotic perforations ("shot-holes") punched through the lamina.
Florida.lesion_morphology_quote: Tan-orange dust-like pustules cover the abaxial surface uniformly, accompanied by a handful of small dark holes where tissue has dropped out.
Florida.affected_organs_quote: A single soybean leaflet is held up showing its lower (abaxial) surface coated with rust pustules; no stem or pod is in frame.
Florida.spread_pattern_quote: Pustules are spread evenly from leaf base to tip and across both halves of the lamina rather than clustered in patches.
variations_from_canonical: Pustules appear as very fine pinpoint specks rather than as discrete 2-5 mm angular gray-green/tan lesions — individual angular lesion outlines are not resolvable.
Several small shot-hole perforations are visible in the leaf, which are not mentioned in the canonical description.
Coverage looks more diffuse and uniform than the canonical 'lower-canopy, scattered angular lesions' pattern, suggesting a later, more confluent sporulation stage.

Alabama.severity_quote: Entire rows of soybean plants in the foreground are uniformly brown, desiccated, and collapsed, indicating whole-plant death rather than localized lesions.
Alabama.lesion_morphology: No discrete lesions are resolvable at this field-scale distance; affected plants show whole-canopy bronzing and tan-brown desiccation rather than individual spots.
Alabama.lesion_morphology_quote: Foliage on the diseased rows reads as a continuous rust-tan to dark-brown wash with no visible spot margins or microsclerotia.
Alabama.affected_organs_quote: Leaves are uniformly browned and shriveled and the stems beneath are bare and stiff, with plants holding their dead foliage upright in the row.
Alabama.spread_pattern: patchy along rows, concentrated near the field edge
Alabama.spread_pattern_quote: The browning is densest in the rows nearest the dirt road/field margin in the foreground, while plants further into the interior of the field remain green.
variations_from_canonical: Image is a wide field shot showing whole-plant premature death and bronzing across rows — none of the canonical close-up diagnostic features (microsclerotia, charcoal-sprinkled epidermis, marbled-gray stem cross-sections, reddish-brown cotyledon spots) are visible or assessable.
Symptom expression here is at a stand/canopy scale (entire rows collapsed) rather than the lesion- and tissue-level signs the canonical text emphasizes.

Distribution is clearly edge-biased / row-patchy in this photo; the canonical summary does not describe spatial spread pattern, so this is additional information the image provides beyond the reference.

Georgia.severity_quote: The stem is dry, woody, and split open to reveal a substantial silver-gray patch of internal tissue, indicating the disease has progressed to the diagnostic microsclerotia-laden phase.
Georgia.lesion_morphology: An elongated, oval silvery-gray patch of exposed inner stem tissue with fine dark speckling, bordered by torn brown epidermis and shredded fibrous bark; the lesion runs longitudinally along the stem axis.
Georgia.lesion_morphology_quote: A roughly lens-shaped gray-silver area with peppered black specks sits within a split in the brown stem, surrounded by frayed bark fibers.
Georgia.affected_organs_quote: The image shows a split soybean stem section with the epidermis peeled back, exposing the inner pith and vascular tissue with characteristic gray discoloration.
Georgia.spread_pattern_quote: The discoloration is concentrated in one longitudinal patch along the exposed inner stem, with adjacent bark areas appearing comparatively normal brown.
variations_from_canonical: Image is a studio/lab specimen photograph of an isolated stem segment on a gray background rather than a field photo, so canopy spread cannot be assessed.
Lesion is shown as a longitudinal split exposing the pith rather than a transverse cross-section, so the canonical 'marbled gray' cross-section pattern is not directly visible.
Microsclerotia speckling is fine and somewhat subtle in this view rather than the strongly contrasting 'charcoal-sprinkled' density the canonical text emphasizes.

Illinois.severity_quote: Several rows in the foreground show large stretches of dead, collapsed, tan-brown plants while adjacent rows remain green and healthy.
Illinois.affected_organs_quote: Affected plants appear entirely tan/brown and collapsed onto the soil, with foliage desiccated and stems wilted along whole row segments.
Illinois.spread_pattern_quote: Dead plants form continuous strips down specific rows while neighboring rows remain green, producing a striped, row-aligned patch pattern across the field.
variations_from_canonical: Image is a wide field-level view showing stand loss, not a close-up; no individual lower-stem dark brown lesion is visible.
Damage appears as long continuous row segments of dead plants rather than discrete scattered patches.
Affected plants are fully collapsed and tan/desiccated rather than showing the canonical 'wilted yellow leaves remaining attached' on still-standing plants.

Iowa.severity_quote: The lower stem shows extensive dark brown discoloration extending well up from the base, with surrounding leaves browned and desiccated, indicating advanced disease progression.
Iowa.lesion_morphology: A continuous dark chocolate-brown to reddish-brown lesion runs along the length of the lower and mid stem, fully encircling and discoloring the stem with a diffuse upper margin where it transitions into the still-green upper stem.
Iowa.lesion_morphology_quote: The stem displays a uniform dark-brown girdling discoloration with fine pubescence still visible over the lesion and no sharp boundary at the upper edge.
Iowa.affected_organs_quote: The main stem is darkened along its full visible length, and surrounding trifoliate leaves are tan-brown, curled, and dried while still attached.
Iowa.spread_pattern_quote: Discoloration runs continuously up the stem from below into the upper canopy region, with browned leaves distributed around the affected stem.
variations_from_canonical: Lesion in this image extends noticeably higher up the stem than the 'lower stem' description in the canonical reference, reaching into the mid/upper stem region.
Wilted leaves visible here are fully tan/dried and curled rather than the yellow-then-brown progression described, suggesting a later stage than the canonical wilt description.
No taproot or root tissue is visible in this close-up frame, so the canonical taproot/secondary-root rotting cannot be confirmed from this image.

Illinois.severity_quote: A central stem is bleached tan-white with white fluffy mycelia at nodes and several small black sclerotia visible, while attached pods have shriveled and dried.
Illinois.lesion_morphology: A bleached tan-to-cream stem lesion runs along the main stem with patches of white cottony mycelium concentrated at nodes and pod attachments, dotted with a few small black sclerotia.
Illinois.lesion_morphology_quote: Stem and pod surfaces show pale tan bleaching with white fluffy fungal growth and small black specks (sclerotia) at nodes.
Illinois.affected_organs_quote: The main stem is bleached and colonized with white mycelium, the attached pods are shriveled and tan, and surrounding leaves are yellowed and necrotic.
Illinois.spread_pattern: localized to a single plant's main stem and adjacent pods
Illinois.spread_pattern_quote: Disease is concentrated on one central diseased stem with healthy green pods on neighboring plants visible to the right.
variations_from_canonical: Sclerotia visible in this image are small and few rather than the prominent rodent-dropping-sized bodies emphasized in the canonical description.
Stem is not yet fully dry and bleached white along its entire length — bleaching is partial and patchy with mycelium still present, suggesting active rather than fully dead/dry stage.
Foliar wilting/graying is not the dominant feature here; the image is centered on stem and pod symptoms rather than the canonical wilted grayish-green leaf appearance.

Iowa.severity_quote: A localized brown stem lesion with a small patch of white mycelium and one dark sclerotium is visible, while surrounding pods and leaves remain green and turgid.
Iowa.lesion_morphology: An elongated tan-to-brown bleached lesion runs along the soybean stem with a small tuft of white cottony mycelium near its upper margin and a single rounded black sclerotium embedded in the lesion surface.
Iowa.lesion_morphology_quote: Brown, slightly sunken stem lesion with a white fluffy mycelial patch and one dark, irregularly rounded sclerotium protruding from the affected bark.
Iowa.affected_organs_quote: The discoloration, mycelium, and sclerotium are confined to the main stem; the attached pubescent pods and leaves visible in frame appear green and unaffected.
Iowa.spread_pattern_quote: Infection is restricted to a single segment of the stem with surrounding green pods still attached and healthy-looking.
variations_from_canonical: Only one sclerotium is visible rather than the multiple rodent-dropping-like sclerotia described canonically.
White fluffy mycelial growth is sparse — a small patch rather than the extensive cottony coverage described for moist conditions.
Pods adjacent to the lesion appear green and healthy with no visible pod or seed infection, despite the canonical reference listing pods and seeds as affected.
Stem is not yet fully bleached white — discoloration is brown/tan rather than the dry bleached appearance of late-stage infection.

Illinois.severity_quote: A single soybean leaflet shows numerous coalescing dark-brown lesions with several lesions where dead tissue has dropped out, leaving irregular tan/cream-colored holes with ragged margins.
Illinois.lesion_morphology: Small to medium angular dark-brown to nearly black lesions, many surrounded by faint yellow-tan halos and pale necrotic tan centers; some lesions have coalesced into larger irregular blotches and dead tissue has fallen out, producing tattered "shot-hole" gaps with ragged brown borders.
Illinois.lesion_morphology_quote: Numerous angular brown spots are scattered across the leaf with thin chlorotic halos, and several lesions have collapsed into pale tan papery centers or torn-out holes outlined by dark-brown necrotic margins.
Illinois.affected_organs_quote: Only a single soybean leaflet (upper surface) is shown, densely speckled with brown lesions and torn necrotic patches; no stems, pods, or seeds are visible in the frame.
Illinois.spread_pattern_quote: Lesions are distributed fairly evenly over the entire visible leaflet — from the margin to the midrib and across both halves — rather than concentrated at the tip, edge, or base.
variations_from_canonical: The 'ragged/shot-hole' stage is more pronounced than the canonical description implies — multiple lesions on this single leaf have already dropped out into irregular holes, suggesting later progression than the 'small angular water-soaked spots' starting stage.
Chlorotic/yellowish-green halos around lesions are subtle here; most spots are dominated by dark-brown necrotic centers rather than the bright yellow-green halos emphasized in the canonical text.
Water-soaked margins are not visible in this image — lesions appear fully necrotic and dry, indicating an older lesion stage rather than the early water-soaked phase described canonically.
Only foliar symptoms are visible; canonical reference also lists stem, pod, and seed involvement, but none of those organs are present in this frame to confirm or deny.

Indiana.severity_quote: A single soybean leaflet held in a hand shows numerous small dark lesions scattered across the blade with several yellow halos and a few small holes where tissue has dropped out, but the leaf is still largely green and intact.
Indiana.lesion_morphology: Many small (roughly 1–3 mm) dark reddish-brown to purplish-brown angular flecks, some clustered into larger patches, with diffuse chlorotic yellow halos around the more advanced lesions and a few centers that have torn out into small irregular holes.
Indiana.lesion_morphology_quote: Tiny dark brown angular spots are densely peppered across the leaflet, with bright yellow halos surrounding clusters of lesions and small ragged holes on the left side of the blade.
Indiana.affected_organs_quote: Only a single detached/held trifoliate leaflet is visible; lesions cover its upper surface and no stems, pods, or seeds are shown.
Indiana.spread_pattern: scattered across leaf blade, concentrated near margins and along veins
Indiana.spread_pattern_quote: Lesions are distributed across the entire leaflet but appear denser toward the leaf margins and clustered between secondary veins, with a few coalesced yellow patches on the left margin.
variations_from_canonical: Lesions in this image read more as small dark flecks/peppering than as the classic large angular water-soaked spots described canonically.
Yellowish-green halos are present but only around a subset of clustered lesions, not surrounding each individual spot as the canonical description implies.

Small holes from dropped-out tissue are already visible even though most lesions are still tiny and discrete, suggesting shot-hole/ragged appearance is occurring earlier in lesion development than the canonical 'spots often merge to form large dead patches' progression.
Only a leaf is shown — no pod, stem, or seed symptoms are visible, so the multi-organ involvement in the canonical description cannot be confirmed from this photograph.

Iowa.severity_quote: Numerous small pale-yellow flecks are scattered across the upper leaf surface but the leaf remains predominantly green with no necrosis or defoliation visible.
Iowa.lesion_morphology: Small, irregular pale-green to light-yellow spots, roughly 2–5 mm across, with diffuse indistinct margins and no visible browning at their centers.
Iowa.lesion_morphology_quote: The upper leaflet shows many faint chlorotic flecks of varied size, often clustered along veins, all still pale yellow without any tan or necrotic centers.
Iowa.affected_organs_quote: Chlorotic flecking is visible across the upper surface of the trifoliate leaflets being held by the hand; no pods, stems, or seeds are shown.
Iowa.spread_pattern_quote: The pale spots are distributed in a scattered, somewhat clustered pattern across the entire upper leaflet rather than concentrated at the margin or tip.
variations_from_canonical: Image shows only the early/chlorotic phase: spots are still pale yellow with no brown centers or yellow halos as the canonical text describes for advanced lesions.
No grayish or pale-purplish sporulation is visible because only the upper (adaxial) leaf surface is shown; the diagnostic underside fuzz cannot be seen in this photo.
Only foliar symptoms are visible — no pods or seeds appear in the frame, so the canonical 'Pod; Seed' affected parts cannot be confirmed from this image.

Kentucky.severity_quote: All three leaflets of the trifoliolate are heavily speckled with numerous coalescing tan-brown lesions, with visible necrosis and small perforations through the lamina.
Kentucky.lesion_morphology: Numerous small, irregular to angular tan-to-grayish-brown spots scattered densely across the leaflets, many with pale tan centers and diffuse darker margins, coalescing in places into larger necrotic patches.
Kentucky.lesion_morphology_quote: Spots are roughly 2–5 mm across, irregularly shaped, tan/grayish in color rather than bright yellow, distributed densely across the leaf surface.
Kentucky.affected_organs_quote: A detached trifoliolate soybean leaf shows extensive lesions on all three leaflets; no pods or seeds are visible in the frame.
Kentucky.spread_pattern_quote: Lesions are distributed fairly evenly across the entire surface of each of the three leaflets rather than concentrated at margins, tips, or veins.
variations_from_canonical: Lesions in this image are predominantly tan to grayish-brown rather than the canonical 'pale green to light yellow' early spots, indicating a more advanced stage than the canonical early description.
Necrosis is extensive enough to have caused small holes/tears in the lamina, which goes beyond the canonical description of yellow spots with brown centers.
Image is a detached leaf on a cardboard backdrop (specimen photograph), not an in-canopy field view, so canopy-level spread and lower-surface sporulation cannot be directly assessed.

No grayish/pale-purplish sporulation tufts are clearly resolvable in this view; the diagnostic lower-surface fuzz described canonically is not visibly distinguishable from the general tan lesion coloration.

Iowa.severity_quote: A patch of plants in the right-center foreground shows pronounced yellowing and bronzing of foliage with apparent leaf necrosis, contrasting sharply with surrounding healthy green canopy.
Iowa.lesion_morphology: Discrete leaf-spot lesions are not resolvable at this distance; affected leaves appear broadly chlorotic-yellow to bronze with patchy brown necrotic areas rather than showing isolated small spots.
Iowa.lesion_morphology_quote: The diseased plants display whole-leaf yellowing and brown discoloration without clearly visible individual pustules at this scale.
Iowa.affected_organs_quote: Foliage of the affected plants is yellow-bronze with brown necrotic patches; pods and seeds are not visible in the image.
Iowa.spread_pattern_quote: Symptoms are concentrated in a discrete cluster of plants in the right-foreground while the surrounding canopy remains predominantly green and healthy.
variations_from_canonical: Image is a wide field-scale view rather than the close-up leaf shot implied by the canonical description, so individual small light-green spots and light-colored pustules cannot be resolved.
Affected foliage appears as broad chlorosis and bronzing with coalesced brown necrosis rather than the discrete small raised-center lesions described canonically.
No pods or seeds are visible, so the canonical mention of pod symptoms cannot be confirmed from this image.
Distribution appears as a localized patch of severely affected plants rather than the diffuse foliar lesion pattern the canonical description suggests.

Kentucky.severity: moderate to advanced (range across sampled leaves)
Kentucky.severity_quote: Four detached leaves laid on cardboard show a progression from a mostly green leaf with scattered small flecks to leaves with extensive coalesced brown necrotic patches, holes/tattering, and pronounced curling.
Kentucky.lesion_morphology: Small brown angular flecks scattered across the laminae that coalesce into larger irregular dark-brown necrotic patches; affected tissue has dropped out in places, producing ragged holes, and the worst leaves are puckered/curled.
Kentucky.lesion_morphology_quote: The two more affected leaves show merged brown blotches with torn margins and shot-hole-like perforations, while the milder leaves carry only pinpoint brown specks on otherwise green tissue.
Kentucky.affected_organs_quote: Only detached trifoliate leaflets are shown on the cardboard next to a 6-inch ruler; no stems, pods, or seeds are present in the frame.
Kentucky.spread_pattern: scattered to coalescing across the lamina (whole-leaf comparison set, not an in-canopy view)
Kentucky.spread_pattern_quote: Lesions are distributed across the entire blade of each leaflet rather than confined to margins or veins, with denser clustering producing the brown coalesced patches on the second and fourth leaves.
variations_from_canonical: Lesions appear brown and necrotic with shot-hole tattering rather than the small light-green spots with raised pale centers described as the early/typical canonical appearance — consistent with the mature stage the canonical also mentions.
Pale raised pustules in the centers of lesions are not resolvable at this whole-leaf scale (the canonical notes 20X magnification is needed).
Pronounced leaf curling/puckering is visible on at least one leaflet, which the canonical text does not list as a primary symptom.
Only foliar tissue is shown; the canonical also lists pod and seed involvement, none of which is visible here.
